content: 2025’s configurable ICM curiosity-driven autonomous UAV swarm agents can be optimized by tying intrinsic reward generation directly to the working memory capacity limits of individual swarm agents: instead of rewarding all novel observations equally, agents only generate intrinsic rewards for novel stimuli that fit within their available working memory, discarding overly complex novel patterns that would exceed their capacity. This creates a self-regulating curiosity dynamic where the swarm collectively explores more efficiently by distributing novel search tasks across agents based on their available memory bandwidth.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
